description.en: Sweden's beloved fika bun: a cardamom-scented dough twisted into a knot, filled with cinnamon and brown sugar, and topped with crunchy pearl sugar. Smaller and less sweet than its American cousin, it's celebrated every October 4 on Kanelbullens dag.
description.de: Schwedens liebstes Fika-Gebäck: ein kardamomduftender Hefeteig, zum Knoten gedreht, mit Zimt und braunem Zucker gefüllt und mit knusprigem Perlzucker bestreut. Kleiner und weniger süß als sein amerikanisches Pendant, gefeiert am 4. Oktober.
description.es: El bollo favorito del fika sueco: una masa perfumada con cardamomo, anudada, rellena de canela y azúcar moreno y coronada con azúcar perlado crujiente. Más pequeño y menos dulce que su primo americano, se celebra cada 4 de octubre.
description.fr: La brioche fétiche du fika suédois : une pâte à la cardamome, nouée, fourrée de cannelle et de sucre roux, couronnée de sucre perlé croquant. Plus petite et moins sucrée que sa cousine américaine, fêtée chaque 4 octobre.

origin.note: A fika staple since the early 20th century; cardamom in the dough is considered essential, not a flourish. Distinct from, and ancestral in spirit to, the larger American cinnamon roll.

ingredients.note: for the tangzhong (cooked flour-milk-water scald that softens the crumb)

keeping.note: Best the day they're baked since there's no icing to seal in moisture; freezes and reheats well.

method.title: Make the tangzhong
method.summary: Whisk flour into the water and milk and cook gently, stirring, until it thickens into a smooth paste, then let it cool.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine flour, yeast, cardamom, salt and sugar, then work in the cooled tangzhong, egg, milk and melted butter until you have a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough rise until roughly doubled, about an hour.

method.title: Fill
method.summary: Roll the dough into a rectangle, spread with soft butter, and scatter the cinnamon and brown sugar evenly over the surface.

method.title: Twist into knots
method.summary: Cut the dough into strips, then twist and loop each one around your fingers into a knot, tucking the tail through the centre.

method.title: Second rise and top
method.summary: Let the shaped buns puff for about 45 minutes, then brush with beaten egg and scatter pearl sugar on top.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Bake hot and fast, around 200-204°C (400°F), for 12-14 minutes, just until set - a few minutes too long will dry them out.

source.adaptation: Tangzhong (cooked flour-liquid scald) is counted into the dough total, not as a preferment since nothing ferments in it. formula.* excludes the filling, egg wash and pearl-sugar topping.
